perturbation.original_text: Boxlot shall have sole control over the user agreement that governs the Service, and Boxlot shall have the sole responsibility for dealing with breaches of such user agreement. However, if theglobe has an issue with the user agreement or Boxlot's enforcement of it, the parties shall discuss such issues in good faith.
perturbation.changed_text: Boxlot shall have sole control over the user agreement that governs the Service, and theglobe shall have the ultimate responsibility for dealing with breaches of such user agreement. However, if theglobe has an issue with the user agreement or Boxlot's enforcement of it, the parties shall discuss such issues in good faith.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that Boxlot has sole control over the user agreement, but then assigns ultimate responsibility for breaches of that agreement to theglobe. This creates a direct conflict of responsibilities. The 'sole control' attributed to Boxlot is now contradicted by 'theglobe's ultimate responsibility', this means that theglobe has the responsiblity to dealing with user breaches within Boxlot platform.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Boxlot shall be responsible for providing all customer support regarding the Service or the Co-Branded Pages, and theglobe may redirect to Boxlot any associated customer support inquiries. Boxlot shall have sole control over the user agreement that governs the Service, and theglobe shall have the ultimate responsibility for dealing with breaches of such user agreement. However, if theglobe has an issue with the user agreement or Boxlot's enforcement of it, the parties shall discuss such issues in good faith. Furthermore, Boxlot's privacy policy shall make any disclosures, or obtain any User consent, reasonably requested by theglobe that the parties believe are necessary to make the disclosures about Users back to theglobe required by this Agreement. All Users shall be treated at least as favorable in all respects (including without limitation with respect to pricing, quality of service, and customer support responsiveness) as Boxlot treats users of the Boxlot Site.
perturbation.justification: The change assigns 'ultimate responsibility' to 'theglobe' for breaches of the user agreement, while the original text assigned sole responsibility for dealing with breaches to Boxlot. The contradicted text repeats this same contradictory assignment of responsibility. This is a direct contradiction, creating uncertainty and potential disputes regarding who is ultimately responsible for enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: theglobe shall provide Boxlot with ad serving code, which Boxlot shall implement at its own expense on all Co-Branded Pages.
perturbation.changed_text: theglobe shall provide Boxlot with ad serving code, which Boxlot shall implement on all Co-Branded Pages, with expenses shared equally between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The change contradicts the original statement that Boxlot is responsible for the implementation at its own expense. The insertion of 'with expenses shared equally between the parties' directly conflicts with Boxlot bearing all costs. This creates uncertainty about the financial responsibilities associated with ad serving implementation. This will lead to another issue in the payment terms section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties shall make the payments described in Exhibit A. Overdue payments shall accrue interest, at the lesser of 1 1/2% per month or the maximum allowable interest under applicable law, from due date until paid, and the owing party shall pay the owed party's costs of collection (including reasonable attorneys' fees).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct financial obligation (expenses shared equally) that contradicts the original clause stating Boxlot is solely responsible for implementation expenses. This directly impacts the contradicted text, which specifies payment terms and interest on overdue amounts, creating ambiguity about how ad serving implementation costs are handled and if they fall under the existing payment structure. The ambiguity created in the first changed text means it contradicts the other clause, triggering the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: Boxlot shall grant to theglobe, pursuant to industry- standard terms negotiated between the parties in good faith, immediately vested options to purchase shares of common stock equal to up to 4% of Boxlot's fully diluted capital stock (in accordance with the chart below).
perturbation.changed_text: Boxlot shall grant to theglobe, pursuant to terms arbitrarily determined by Boxlot, immediately vested options to purchase shares of common stock equal to up to 4% of Boxlot's fully diluted capital stock (in accordance with the chart below).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'industry-standard terms negotiated between the parties in good faith' with 'terms arbitrarily determined by Boxlot'. This creates an internal contradiction because the original phrase implies mutual agreement and fairness, while the modified phrase suggests unilateral control and potential unfairness, especially regarding the Equity Stake of the deal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Boxlot shall grant to theglobe, pursuant to terms arbitrarily determined by Boxlot, immediately vested options to purchase shares of common stock equal to up to 4% of Boxlot's fully diluted capital stock (in accordance with the chart below). Theglobe shall be restricted from exercising such options until the first anniversary of the Effective Date, and such options shall have a five-year term. Each such option shall have an exercise price equal to the lesser of (1) the price at which Boxlot's common stock is sold to the public in an initial public offering or (2) $6 per share; each of the foregoing as may be adjusted for any stock splits, combinations or re-organizations affecting Boxlot's capital stock. Boxlot shall grant to theglobe registration rights for such options and any shares of common stock issued or issuable upon the exercise of such options (including without limitation, two demand registration rights and unlimited piggyback registration rights) on Form S-1, Form S-3 or such other form as may be applicable pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933 as amended.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'industry-standard terms negotiated between the parties in good faith' with 'terms arbitrarily determined by Boxlot.' This directly contradicts the expectation of fair negotiation and mutual agreement, creating a significant inconsistency. The contradicted text relies on this arbitrary determination, which undermines the implied fairness and enforceability that is typically expected in contractual agreements, thereby causing contradiction. If terms are arbitrarily determined, it is uncertain if any negotiation actually occurred or if the globe agreed to such terms, which leads to unenforceability.
